questions.clue: Used for this, like game-changing meds of the 2020s, the 1990s drug cocktail fen-phen had issues & a $3 billion-plus settlement ensued
The USA's Master Settlement Agreement was signed in 1998 by 52 attorneys general & the 4 largest cos. in this industry
This Wolfsburg company had to compensate thousands of drivers after cheating on emissions tests in "Dieselgate"
In 2026 the family of this woman reached a settlement with a pharma company that used her "Immortal" cells without consent

This attorney has won settlements in several police misconduct cases such as the death of Sonya Massey
Jumpman was the original name of this iconic character who debuted in 1981, a couple of years before his bro
George R.R. Martin helped create the backstory for the game in which players attempt to restore this shattered ring
San Andreas in this long-running game series is a crime-ridden fictional state, not a seismic fault
This 3-word title comes before "Warzone", "Modern Warfare" & more
An award-winning 2009 game saw Batman facing down the Joker & other villains in this title institution
Before an activist named Clara Driscoll stepped up in 1903, this site where Jim Bowie fought was in danger of being replaced by a hotel
The West Texas city of Odessa is home to replicas of Stonehenge & this 16th century London theatre
Named for a pair of cities, this airport ranks among the busiest in the nation with about 86 million annual travelers
During WWI the German-settled town of New Brandenburg changed its name to this nickname for the U.S. flag
The Bridge of the Americas & the Stanton-Lerdo Bridge connect Juárez, Mexico to this border city
In 622 Muhammad & his followers made the journey known as the Hijrah, fleeing to Medina from this city
"Feudal" was coined in the 1600s from Latin "feudum", meaning this type of land parcel on which the feudal system was built

When completed in 1144, Paris' Abbey of Saint-Denis was the first major example of this architectural style that would become all the rage
France's confiscation of the English-held duchy of Guyenne kicked off this conflict  that went into O.T. after O.T.
In 979 this dynasty with a musical-sounding name completed its reunification of China

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Octavia Spencer presents the clue.) The FBI teamed with Philadelphia police to catch a cold-blooded killer for hire who called himself the next this after a hitman played by Keanu Reeves; unlike in the movies, the real-life killer got five consecutive life sentences
Operation Oxy Alley took down fake pain-management clinics that distributed 20 million pills of the opioid known by this generic name

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Octavia Spencer presents the clue.) Reports of drugs, smuggling, & sex inside a Baltimore jail led to the conviction of dozens of inmates & corrections officers for this type of organized criminal conspiracy, spelled out in federal RICO statutes
In Illinois, FBI agents couldn't act until a suspect fled a traffic stop, giving this 2-word principle to justify searching his home

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Octavia Spencer presents the clue.) In "Operation Mean Streets", an undercover informant risks his life to infiltrate the infamously violent gang, originally called Mara Salvatrucha but better known by this alphanumeric name
Around 1000 A.D. Mahmud of Ghazna was the 1st Muslim ruler to be called this title; this "of Swat" was a much different guy
Time to throw you (figuratively!) under this female type of promiscuous demon
Give it to me straight--don't do this 9-letter verb that means to try to sweeten an unpleasant situation
It's the solid white fat around sheep's kidneys
This adjective means beneath the collarbone

final.clue: Adopted in 1979, this name reflected size & strength as well as a promoter's wish to appeal to Irish-American fans

Near an English settlement called a hillfort is the Uffington White this, carved into a chalk hillside 3,000 years ago
Semiyarka or "7 Ravines" is a newly studied Bronze Age settlement in the Kazakh this, a tree-deprived type of Eurasian plain

[IMAGE CLUE]
What UNESCO calls the oldest center of civilization in the Americas, Caral-Supe is a settlement in these mountains
Around 120 B.C. Narbo Martius, the modern town of Narbonne, was Rome's first colony in the land the Romans called this

[IMAGE CLUE]
Jorge Álvares of Portugal was an early visitor to China, leading to this first European settlement in the Far East
This compound adjective has traditionally, though not exclusively, described a certain tenacious spider
Tread carefully: literally or figuratively, it's an area fraught with unseen hazards
"The whole" one means the entirety; split the word & add an "S" & you've got a Ricky Martin song title
Often accompanied by pointing, it's a folksy way to say "in the direction indicated"
If somebody describes you as this word meaning related to the theater, it's usually not a compliment
Part 3 of this Ray Bradbury classic is titled "Burning Bright"
After this most famous novel, E.L. Doctorow moved up in time a few decades to the Depression in "Loon Lake"
The best known in a series of books, it came between "Farmer Boy" & "On the Banks of Plum Creek" in order of publication
Amy Tan dedicated this 1989 bestseller "to my mother and the memory of her mother"
Norman Mailer chronicled the life, crimes & death of convicted killer Gary Gilmore in this 1980 Pulitzer Prize winner
This Australian city named for a scientist is used in climatology circles for sea level pressure measurements

Centered on 180 degrees west & 50 degrees north, an area of low pressure is named for these islands
The "N" in the O.N.I., or Oceanic this Index, stands for this Spanish word in a name referring to warmer waters

This warm current that starts in the tropical Caribbean carries about 400 times more water than the Amazon
Merchant sailors depended on these steady breezes that blow easterly in equatorial tropical regions

"I Hope I Get It" &"Dance: Ten, Looks: Three" typify the hunger in this show
Joel Goldes coached the cast in Newfoundland dialect for this musical

Angus MacGuffie is a character in this musical about a disappearing & reappearing town

categories.name: IT'S THE ONE-LETTER LAST NAME OF...

questions.clue: The man who founded Boston Temple No. 11 in the 1950s before moving to No. 7 in Harlem
The musician who somehow took a jazz album to No. 2 on both the Billboard 200 and R&B/Hip-Hop charts
The historic figure in the N.Y. Post headline that reported her "childhood home asks $44M at 740 Park Ave" in 2014
The co-founder of a self-help group whose wife Lois co-founded Al-Anon
The 1925 literary character who "was informed by telephone that there would be a small hearing concerning his case"
